gaps.snippet: # [doc = " Runtime, queryable metadata (RFC-0001 §4.3). Fields are private; the only way to build a `Meta`"] # [doc = " is [`Meta::new`], which enforces M-I1…M-I4 — so an inconsistent `Meta` is unrepresentable."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " `reconstruction` (RFC-0003 §6; M-260) is attached via [`Meta::with_reconstruction`] — its own"] # [doc = " invariants are enforced by [`ReconInfo::new`], and it does not interact with M-I1…M-I4."] # [derive (Debug , Clone , PartialEq)] pub struct Meta { provenance : Provenance , guarantee : GuaranteeStrength , bound : Option < Bound > , sparsity : Option < SparsityObs > , physical : Option < PhysicalLayout > , reconstruction : Option < Box < ReconInfo > > , policy_used : Option < ContentHash > , # [doc = " The certification mode this value was produced under (RFC-0034 §3.1; M-786). A **never-silent**"] # [doc = " tag — every `Meta` carries one — defaulting to [`CertMode::Fast`] (RFC-0034 §5). Like all of"] # [doc = " `Meta`, it is **excluded from the content hash** (RFC-0001 §4.6; ADR-003 — switching modes never"] # [doc = " perturbs identity); the exclusion is by construction (the hasher never reads `Meta`)."] cert_mode : CertMode , # [doc = " The explicit Axis-B `wrapping` opt-out, if the producing operation was given a named,"] # [doc = " explicit `wrapping` annotation (RFC-0034 §10; M-791). **Absent by default** — never-silent"] # [doc = " failability (out-of-range → `Option`/`Result`/`SwapError`) is the default in every mode."] # [doc = " Presence means the developer *explicitly* opted in to wraparound at the use site; it is"] # [doc = " never an ambient default and does **not** silence Axis-A guarantee/cert_mode tags (VR-5)."] # [doc = " Excluded from the content hash (rides `Meta`; RFC-0001 §4.6)."] wrapping_opt : Option < WrappingOpt > , }

gaps.snippet: impl Meta { # [doc = " Build a `Meta`, enforcing the guarantee/bound invariants:"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " - **M-I1** `guarantee == Exact ⟺ bound == None`,"] # [doc = " - **M-I2** `Proven ⟹ basis ProvenThm`, **M-I3** `Empirical ⟹ EmpiricalFit`,"] # [doc = "   **M-I4** `Declared ⟹ UserDeclared`,"] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " plus numeric well-formedness of any bound. Returns [`WfError`] on violation."] pub fn new (provenance : Provenance , guarantee : GuaranteeStrength , bound : Option < Bound > , sparsity : Option < SparsityObs > , physical : Option < PhysicalLayout > , policy_used : Option < ContentHash > ,) -> Result < Self , WfError > { check_guarantee_bound (guarantee , bound . as_ref ()) ? ; if let Some (b) = & bound { if ! b . well_formed () { return Err (WfError :: MalformedBound) ; } } if let Some (s) = & sparsity { if ! (0.0 ..= 1.0) . contains (& s . density) { return Err (WfError :: MalformedSparsity) ; } } Ok (Meta { provenance , guarantee , bound , sparsity , physical , reconstruction : None , policy_used , cert_mode : CertMode :: default () , wrapping_opt : None , }) } # [doc = " Attach a reconstruction manifest (RFC-0003 §6; M-260). The manifest's own schema"] # [doc = " invariants are already enforced by [`ReconInfo::new`]; it is independent of M-I1…M-I4,"] # [doc = " so this cannot invalidate an existing `Meta`."] # [must_use] pub fn with_reconstruction (mut self , reconstruction : ReconInfo) -> Self { self . reconstruction = Some (Box :: new (reconstruction)) ; self } # [doc = " Record the schedule-staged packing chosen at a lowering stage (RFC-0004 §5; DN-01;"] # [doc = " M-250). This is the **inspectable record** of the layout decision, not the decision locus"] # [doc = " (the selector is [`mycelium-select`](https://docs.rs/mycelium-select); RFC-0005 §4)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **M-I5 (lossless `physical`).** The layout is a lossless re-encoding of the same `payload`:"] # [doc = " it touches only the `physical` field and leaves the guarantee, bound, and value untouched"] # [doc = " — so recording (or *re*-recording) it can never change the value's type or its guarantee"] # [doc = " (RFC-0001 §4.3; `physical-layout.schema.json`). M-I1…M-I4 are therefore preserved by"] # [doc = " construction."] # [must_use] pub fn with_physical (mut self , physical : PhysicalLayout) -> Self { self . physical = Some (physical) ; self } # [doc = " Record the certification mode this value was produced under (RFC-0034 §3.1; M-786). Touches"] # [doc = " only the `cert_mode` tag — it leaves the guarantee, bound, and value untouched, so it can"] # [doc = " never change a value's strength or identity (the mode rides `Meta`, excluded from the content"] # [doc = " hash; RFC-0001 §4.6 / ADR-003). A stronger mode is **not** a stronger guarantee (VR-5)."] # [must_use] pub fn with_cert_mode (mut self , cert_mode : CertMode) -> Self { self . cert_mode = cert_mode ; self } # [doc = " Attach the explicit named Axis-B `wrapping` opt-out marker (RFC-0034 §10; M-791)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Touches only `wrapping_opt` — leaves the guarantee, bound, cert_mode, and value untouched."] # [doc = " The opt-out is **never-silent**: the developer explicitly called this method at the use site,"] # [doc = " making the intent visible and grep-auditable. It does **not** silence Axis-A guarantee tags"] # [doc = " or the certification mode; those axes are orthogonal (VR-5 — opting out of one axis never"] # [doc = " upgrades another)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **Absent by default:** not calling this method means Axis-B never-silent failability is"] # [doc = " active (the safe default). Presence signals \"the caller explicitly requested wraparound.\""] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **FLAG (M-791):** the op-layer wiring that *honors* this marker by actually electing"] # [doc = " wraparound instead of returning `Option`/`Result`/`SwapError` is a downstream task"] # [doc = " (M-788 onward); this method records the intent at the metadata level."] # [must_use] pub fn with_wrapping (mut self , opt : WrappingOpt) -> Self { self . wrapping_opt = Some (opt) ; self } # [doc = " The common `Exact` metadata with no bound (M-I1)."] # [must_use] pub fn exact (provenance : Provenance) -> Self { Meta { provenance , guarantee : GuaranteeStrength :: Exact , bound : None , sparsity : None , physical : None , reconstruction : None , policy_used : None , cert_mode : CertMode :: default () , wrapping_opt : None , } } # [doc = " The value's provenance."] # [must_use] pub fn provenance (& self) -> & Provenance { & self . provenance } # [doc = " The disclosed guarantee strength."] # [must_use] pub fn guarantee (& self) -> GuaranteeStrength { self . guarantee } # [doc = " The bound, if approximate."] # [must_use] pub fn bound (& self) -> Option < & Bound > { self . bound . as_ref () } # [doc = " Measured sparsity, if recorded."] # [must_use] pub fn sparsity (& self) -> Option < SparsityObs > { self . sparsity } # [doc = " The recorded physical layout, if any."] # [must_use] pub fn physical (& self) -> Option < PhysicalLayout > { self . physical } # [doc = " The reconstruction manifest, if attached (RFC-0003 §6)."] # [must_use] pub fn reconstruction (& self) -> Option < & ReconInfo > { self . reconstruction . as_deref () } # [doc = " The policy that produced this value (set iff produced by a swap)."] # [must_use] pub fn policy_used (& self) -> Option < & ContentHash > { self . policy_used . as_ref () } # [doc = " The certification mode this value was produced under (RFC-0034 §3.1; M-786). Always present —"] # [doc = " a never-silent tag — defaulting to [`CertMode::Fast`]."] # [must_use] pub fn cert_mode (& self) -> CertMode { self . cert_mode } # [doc = " The explicit Axis-B `wrapping` opt-out marker, if present (RFC-0034 §10; M-791)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Returns `None` (the default) when Axis-B never-silent failability is active — the safe"] # [doc = " path requires no annotation. Returns `Some(WrappingOpt)` when the producing operation"] # [doc = " was explicitly annotated with a `wrapping` opt-out at the use site."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " Presence does **not** imply a weaker guarantee or a lower cert_mode; those axes are"] # [doc = " orthogonal (VR-5). gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The wire projection of [`Meta`] (`meta.schema.json`). Optional fields are omitted when absent"] # [doc = " (so `Exact` emits no `bound`, satisfying M-I1's presence model); on the way back in, `null` and"] # [doc = " absent both decode to `None`. `reconstruction` (RFC-0003 §6) **is** carried (serialized when"] # [doc = " present, re-validated on the way in). `deny_unknown_fields` makes the schema's"] # [doc = " `additionalProperties: false` a real contract — an unknown wire field is rejected, not silently"] # [doc = " dropped (A6-02/B2-03)."] # [doc = ""] # [doc = " **`cert_mode` is not carried on the wire (M-786, deferred — not silent).** The certification mode"] # [doc = " (RFC-0034 §3.1) is a *runtime* tag resolved from the active `@certification` scope (M-790), not an"] # [doc = " intrinsic property of a persisted value, so it is intentionally **not** part of this projection"] # [doc = " (keeping `meta.schema.json` / `additionalProperties: false` unchanged until the schema migration"] # [doc = " lands). A deserialized `Meta` therefore resolves to [`CertMode::Fast`] — the *weakest* mode, which"] # [doc = " never over-claims a stronger one (the VR-5 floor: a loaded value is never silently `Certified`)."] # [derive (Serialize , Deserialize)] # [serde (deny_unknown_fields)] struct MetaWire { provenance : Provenance , guarantee : GuaranteeStrength , # [serde (default , skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")] bound : Option < Bound > , # [serde (default , skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")] sparsity : Option < SparsityObs > , # [serde (default , skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")] physical : Option < PhysicalLayout > , # [serde (default , skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")] reconstruction : Option < Box < ReconInfo > > , # [serde (default , skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")] policy_used : Option < ContentHash > , }

gaps.snippet: # [doc = " The M-I1…M-I4 guarantee/bound consistency check."] fn check_guarantee_bound (g : GuaranteeStrength , bound : Option < & Bound >) -> Result < () , WfError > { use GuaranteeStrength :: { Declared , Empirical , Exact , Proven } ; let basis_ok = | b : Option < & Bound > , want_proven : bool , want_empirical : bool | match b . map (| b | & b . basis) { Some (BoundBasis :: ProvenThm { .. }) => want_proven , Some (BoundBasis :: EmpiricalFit { .. }) => want_empirical , Some (BoundBasis :: UserDeclared) => ! want_proven && ! want_empirical , None => false , } ; match g { Exact => { if bound . is_none () { Ok (()) } else { Err (WfError :: GuaranteeBoundMismatch) } } Proven if basis_ok (bound , true , false) => Ok (()) , Empirical if basis_ok (bound , false , true) => Ok (()) , Declared if basis_ok (bound , false , false) => Ok (()) , _ => Err (WfError :: GuaranteeBoundMismatch) , } }
